papers.title_en: Graph-ESBMC-PLC: Formal Verification of Graphical PLCopen XML Ladder Diagram Programs Using SMT-Based Model Checking
papers.authors: Pierre Dantas, Lucas Cordeiro, Waldir Junior
papers.evidence: 基于SMT的硬件PLC程序形式验证
papers.tldr: ESBMC-PLC原仅支持文本梯形图格式，图形PLCopen XML导出导致空GOTO中间表示，造成虚假验证成功。Graph-ESBMC-PLC采用DFS遍历图形连接图，提取布尔触点路径作为回路逻辑，并应用三层I/O推断与线圈顺序处理，将图形表示转换为完整中间表示。在3个图形程序上验证，均生成有效GOTO IR且安全验证成功（k=2, <70ms），11个文本基准无回归。该方法填补了图形梯形图形式验证的空白，且不改动验证后端。
papers.abstract_en: PLCopen XML defines two encoding formats for IEC 61131-3 Ladder Diagram programs: a textual encoding using <rung> elements, and a graphical encoding that represents rung logic as a directed graph of localId/refLocalId connections. ESBMC-PLC supported the textual format but parsed graphical exports from CONTROLLINO, Beremiz, and OpenPLC Editor into an empty GOTO intermediate representation, causing vacuous verification success. This paper presents Graph-ESBMC-PLC, which closes this gap with a DFS-based graphical LD resolver. The resolver traverses the connection graph from leftPowerRail to each coil, extracts rung paths as Boolean contact conjunctions, and applies a three-tier I/O inference scheme. Ordering coils by rightPowerRail connectionPointIn sequence ensures SET coils process before RESET coils, matching IEC scan-cycle semantics. The graphical-to-IR conversion leaves the ESBMC backend unchanged. Validation on 3 graphical LD programs from CONTROLLINO/OpenPLC Editor shows all produce full GOTO IR with nondeterministic inputs and rung logic, versus the empty IR previously. All 3 verify SAFE at k=2 under 70ms. The 11 textual LD benchmarks are fully preserved, with no regression. Two Beremiz examples with no LD content or unsupported timer semantics are reported as discovered limitations. Artifact at Zenodo (DantasCordeiro2026graphical, doi:10.5281/zenodo.20699856).

papers.title_en: Process-Verified Reinforcement Learning for Theorem Proving via Lean
papers.evidence: 利用Lean的细粒度验证信号训练RL进行定理证明，直接服务于形式验证。
papers.tldr: 强化学习从可验证奖励通常仅依赖单一二进制信号，而符号证明器提供细粒度结构化反馈。本文利用Lean证明助手作为符号过程Oracle，在训练时提供策略级验证反馈，并融入GRPO风格RL目标。在STP-Lean和DeepSeek-Prover-V1.5上，策略级监督在多数设置中优于仅结果基线，在MiniF2F和ProofNet上取得改进。证明器不仅是评估时的验证器，还可作为训练时的过程奖励Oracle，结合语言模型可扩展性和符号验证可靠性。
papers.abstract_en: While reinforcement learning from verifiable rewards (RLVR) typically has relied on a single binary verification signal, symbolic proof assistants in formal reasoning offer rich, fine-grained structured feedback. This gap between structured processes and unstructured rewards highlights the importance of feedback that is both dense and sound. In this work, we demonstrate that the Lean proof assistant itself can serve as a symbolic process oracle, supplying both outcome-level and fine-grained tactic-level verified feedback during training. Proof attempts are parsed into tactic sequences, and Lean's elaboration marks both locally sound steps and the earliest failing step, yielding dense, verifier-grounded credit signals rooted in type theory. We incorporate these structured rewards into a GRPO-style reinforcement learning objective with first-error propagation and first-token credit methods that balances outcome- and process-level advantages. Experiments with STP-Lean and DeepSeek-Prover-V1.5 show that tactic-level supervision outperforms outcome-only baselines in most settings, delivering improvements on benchmarks such as MiniF2F and ProofNet. Beyond empirical gains, our study highlights a broader perspective: symbolic proof assistants are not only verifiers at evaluation time, but can also act as process-level reward oracles during training. This opens a path toward reinforcement learning frameworks that combine the scalability of language models with the reliability of symbolic verification for formal reasoning.

papers.title_en: The Correctness Illusion in LLM-Generated GPU Kernels
papers.evidence: 模糊测试用于LLM生成GPU内核的正确性验证
papers.tldr: 现有LLM生成GPU内核的正确性评估依赖固定形状的小样本allclose检查，存在正确性幻觉。本文构建包含24个内核的受控语料库（含正确和含LLM转录错误变体），采用op-schema感知的种子化模糊测试结合高精度CPU参考和逐操作容差，精确识别所有错误内核，且零精度损失。扩展至5种GPU上26个操作，结果一致，证实了所有固定形状检查的幻觉，揭示了当前基准测试的局限性。
papers.abstract_en: Benchmarks for LLM-generated GPU kernels (KernelBench, TritonBench, GEAK) score correctness through fixed-shape, small-sample allclose-style checks. The number of inputs varies between benchmarks. The shape, dtype, and tolerance are fixed for each kernel. We test that oracle empirically. We construct a controlled corpus of 24 Triton and CPU stand-in kernels (15 correct controls and 9 LLM-style buggy variants seeded with documented transcription errors) and re-evaluate it under op-schema-aware seeded fuzzing with a high-precision (fp64) CPU reference and per-(op, dtype) absolute tolerances. The seeded oracle flags 9 of 9 buggy kernels and passes 15 of 15 correct controls, at zero precision cost on controls. We extend the corpus to 26 ops (adding a flash-attention pair) and re-run the same protocol on five GPU classes (RTX 3060, A10, L40S, A100 SXM4, H100 NVL). The verdicts are identical across all five GPUs: 10 of 10 illusions caught and 16 of 16 controls clean. The corpus result is about LLM-style transcription bugs that the allclose-on-one-shape oracle certifies as correct, not about the bug rate of any specific deployed LLM. Every flagged failure replays byte-for-byte from a stored seed.

papers.title_en: Witnesses and Counterexamples for Timed Bisimulation
papers.evidence: 时间自动机验证，使用区域图和虚拟时钟，可应用于硬件时序分析
papers.tldr: 时间自动机是实时系统建模的形式化方法，但定时迹等价不可判定，而定时互模拟等价可判定。现有检查工具不提供解释，本文利用虚拟时钟扩展的区域图实现定时互模拟检查。若等价成立，可从符号表示推导所有有效证人；若不等价，可获得反例明确行为差异。这些证人/反例可作为系统精化阶段的测试用例，提升验证实用性。
papers.abstract_en: Timed automata provide a modeling formalism for time-critical properties of reactive systems with discrete-state/continuous-time behaviors. To handle the infinite state space of timed automata, recent verification tools use zone graphs, a symbolic semantic model that guarantees sound results, at least for properties reducible to reachability problems. If we instead want to compare the behavior of two timed automata, checking for timed trace equivalence is undecidable. Fortunately, timed bisimulation equivalence is decidable, but currently available checks do not provide useful explanations of the results. To overcome this limitation, we use a recently proposed extension of zone graphs by so-called virtual clocks. The extension not only facilitates effective tool support for timed bisimilarity checking but also enables the derivation of useful explanations from the results. If timed bisimilarity holds, all witnesses derivable from the composed symbolic representation of both models are indeed valid for both models. If timed bisimilarity does not hold, we describe how to obtain counterexamples, making explicit the behavioral differences. These witnesses/counterexamples may serve as test cases in later stages of system refinement.

papers.title_en: Verifying LTL for Infinite State Systems via Termination Analysis
papers.authors: Nils Lommen, Moritz Leven Rosarius, Jürgen Giesl
papers.evidence: 通过终止分析验证无限状态系统的线性时序逻辑属性
papers.tldr: 针对无限状态系统的LTL模型检查问题，本文提出MoAT框架，将LTL模型检查归约为公平终止问题，并利用现有的终止分析工具KoAT和LoAT进行验证。实验表明，MoAT与现有最先进的工具性能相当，展示了终止分析技术在该领域的有效性。
papers.abstract_en: We show that existing tools for termination analysis are extremely well suited for LTL model checking of infinite state systems. To this end, we present a framework MoAT which uses the well-known automata-based approach and reduces the LTL model checking problem to fair termination. To prove or disprove fair termination, it then calls the termination tools KoAT and LoAT in the backend. Our experiments show that in this way, MoAT is on par with existing state-of-the-art tools for LTL model checking of infinite state systems.
